title: Cyclobalanopsis camusiae
taxonomy: Cyclobalanopsis camusiae is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family Fagaceae. It is classified within the genus Cyclobalanopsis, a group of beech species that are primarily found in Asia. The genus Cyclobalanopsis is closely related to the genus Castanea, which includes sweet chestnut species. The classification of Cyclobalanopsis camusiae is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its ovate leaves and small acorns. Within the Fagaceae family, Cyclobalanopsis camusiae is a unique species that is distinct from other beech species.

intro: Cyclobalanopsis camusiae, also known as Camus's chestnut oak, is a species of deciduous tree in the beech family (Fagaceae) that is native to southern China. The health benefits of Cyclobalanopsis camusiae include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health due to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The bioactive constituents of this plant include triterpenoids, such as ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Cyclobalanopsis camusiae is often used in traditional Chinese medicine and is typically prepared as a decoction or extract, or is used as a component in herbal formulations such as capsules or teas.
